|             | RECOMMENDATION 51                                                                                                                                                    | RECOMMENDATION 52                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | RECOMMENDATION 53                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | RECOMMENDATION 54                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DESCRIPTION | The Remuneration Committee should<br>consult with the Chairman and Chief<br>Executive, especially on matters relating<br>to executive Directors and senior officers. | The rules of performance and membership of<br>supervision and control committees should be set out in<br>the board of directors' regulations and aligned with<br>those governing legally mandatory board committees as<br>specified in the preceding sets of recommendations.<br>They should include:<br>a. Committees should be formed exclusively by non-<br>executive Directors, with a majority of<br>independents.<br>b. Committees should be chaired by an independent<br>Director.<br>c. The board should appoint the members of such<br>committees with regard to the knowledge, skills<br>and experience of its directors and each<br>committee's missions, discuss their proposal sand<br>reports; and provide report-backs on their<br>activities and work at the first board plenary<br>following each committee meeting.<br>d. They may engage external advice, when they feel it<br>necessary for the discharge of their functions.<br>e. Meeting proceedings should be minuted and a<br>copy made available to all Board members. | The task of supervising compliance with the<br>policies and rules of the company in the<br>environmental, social and corporate<br>governance areas, and internal rules of<br>conduct, should be assigned to one board<br>committee or split between several, which<br>could be the Audit Committee, the<br>Appointments Committee, a committee<br>specialising in sustainability or corporate<br>social responsibility, or a dedicated<br>committee established by the board under<br>its powers of self-organisation. Such a<br>committee should be made up solely of non-<br>executive directors, the majority being<br>independent and specifically assigned the<br>following minimum functions. | The minimum functions referred to in the previous<br>recommendation are as follows:<br>a. Monitor compliance with the company's internal codes<br>of conduct and corporate governance rules, and<br>ensure that the corporate culture is aligned with its<br>purpose and values.<br>b. Monitor the implementation of the general policy<br>regarding the disclosure of economic-financial, non<br>financial and corporate information, as well as<br>communication with shareholders and investors, proxy<br>advisors and other stakeholders. Similarly, the way in<br>which the entity communicates and relates with small<br>and medium-sized shareholders should be monitored.<br>c. Periodically evaluate the effectiveness of the<br>company's corporate governance system and<br>environmental and social policy, to confirm that it is<br>fulfilling its mission to promote the corporate interest<br>and catering, as appropriate, to the legitimate interests<br>of remaining stakeholders.<br>d. Ensure the company's environmental and social<br>practices are in accordance with the established<br>strategy and policy.<br>e. Monitor and evaluate the company's interaction with<br>its stakeholder groups. |
| COMPLIANT   | Yes                                                                                                                                                                  | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| COMMENTS    |                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

|                       | RECOMMENDATION 55                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | RECOMMENDATION 56                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | RECOMMENDATION 57                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | RECOMMENDATION 58                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|-----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DESCRIPTION           | Environmental and social sustainability policies<br>should identify and include at least:<br>a. The principles, commitments, objectives and<br>strategy regarding shareholders, employees,<br>clients, suppliers, social welfare issues, the<br>environment, diversity, fiscal responsibility,<br>respect for human rights and the prevention<br>of corruption and other illegal conducts.<br>b. The methods or systems for monitoring<br>compliance with policies, associated risks<br>and their management.<br>c. The mechanisms for supervising non-<br>financial risk, including that related to ethical<br>aspects and business conduct.<br>d. Channels for stakeholder communication,<br>participation and dialogue.<br>e. Responsible communication practices that<br>prevent the manipulation of information and<br>protect the company's honour and integrity. | Director remuneration should be<br>sufficient to attract individuals with the<br>desired profile and compensate the<br>commitment, abilities and responsibility<br>that the post demands, but not so high<br>as to compromise the independent<br>judgement of non-executive Directors. | Variable remuneration linked to the company and<br>the Director's performance, the award of shares,<br>options or any other right to acquire shares or to<br>be remunerated on the basis of share price<br>movements, and membership of long-term<br>savings schemes such as pension plans should be<br>confined to executive Directors.<br>The company may consider the share-based<br>remuneration of non-executive Directors provided<br>they retain such shares until the end of their<br>mandate. The above condition will not apply to any<br>shares that the Director must dispose of to defray<br>costs related to their acquisition. | In the case of variable awards, remuneration policies should<br>include limits and technical safeguards to ensure they reflect<br>the professional performance of the beneficiaries and not<br>simply the general progress of the markets or the company's<br>sector, or circumstances of that kind.<br>In particular, variable remuneration items should meet the<br>following conditions:<br>a. Be subject to predetermined and measurable<br>performance criteria that factor the risk assumed to<br>obtain a given outcome.<br>b. Promote the long-term sustainability of the company<br>and include non-financial criteria that are relevant for<br>the company's long-term value, such as compliance<br>with its internal rules and procedures and its risk<br>control and management policies.<br>c. Be focused on achieving a balance between the delivery<br>of short, medium and long-term objectives, such that<br>performance-related pay rewards ongoing<br>achievement, maintained over sufficient time to<br>appreciate its contribution to long-term value creation.<br>This will ensure that performance measurement is not<br>based solely on one-off, occasional or extraordinary<br>events. |
| COMMENTS<br>COMPLIANT | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |

|             | RECOMMENDATION 59                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | RECOMMENDATION 60                                                                                                                                     | RECOMMENDATION 61                                                                                                                                                     |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DESCRIPTION | The payment of the variable components of remuneration is subject to sufficient verification that previously established performance, or other, conditions have been effectively met. Entities should include in their annual directors' remuneration report the criteria relating to the time required and methods for such verification, depending on the nature and characteristics of each variable component. Additionally, entities should consider establishing a reduction clause ('malus') based on deferral for a sufficient period of the payment of part of the variable components that implies total or partial loss of this remuneration in the event that prior to the time of payment an event occurs that makes this advisable. | In the case of remuneration linked to company earnings, deductions should be computed for any qualifications stated in the external auditor's report. | A major part of executive Directors' variable remuneration should be linked to the award of shares or financial instruments whose value is linked to the share price. |
| COMPLIANT   | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Yes                                                                                                                                                   | Yes                                                                                                                                                                   |
| COMMENTS    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                       |

|             | RECOMMENDATION 62                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | RECOMMENDATION 63                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | RECOMMENDATION 64                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
|-------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| DESCRIPTION | Following the award of shares, options or financial<br>instruments corresponding to the remuneration schemes,<br>executive directors should not be able to transfer their<br>ownership or exercise them until a period of at least three<br>years has elapsed. Except for the case in which the director<br>maintains, at the time of the transfer or exercise, a net<br>economic exposure to the variation in the price of the shares<br>for a market value equivalent to an amount of at least twice<br>his or her fixed annual remuneration through the ownership<br>of shares, options or other financial instruments. The<br>foregoing shall not apply to the shares that the director<br>needs to dispose of to meet the costs related to their<br>acquisition or, upon favourable assessment of the<br>Appointments and Remuneration Committee, to address an<br>extraordinary situation. | Contractual arrangements<br>should include provisions<br>that permit the company<br>to reclaim variable<br>components of<br>remuneration when<br>payment was out of step<br>with the Director's actual<br>performance or based on<br>data subsequently found<br>to be misstated. | Termination payments should not exceed a fixed amount equivalent to two years of the Director's total annual<br>remuneration and should not be paid until the company confirms that he or she has met the predetermined<br>performance criteria.<br>For the purposes of this recommendation, payments for contractual termination include any payments whose accrual<br>or payment obligation arises as a consequence of or on the occasion of the termination of the contractual relationship<br>that linked the Director with the company, including previously unconsolidated amounts for long-term savings<br>schemes and the amounts paid under post-contractual non-compete agreements.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| COMPLIANT   | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Yes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Partial compliance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| COMMENTS    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Payments for termination or expiry of the Chairman's and CEO's contracts, including severance pay in the event of<br>termination or expiry of the relationship in certain cases and the post-contractual non-competition agreement, do not<br>exceed the amount equivalent to two years of the total annual remuneration for each of them.<br>In addition, CaixaBank has recognised a social security supplement for the CEO to cover retirement, death and<br>permanent total, absolute or severe disability, and for the Chairman to cover death and permanent total, absolute or<br>severe disability.<br>In the case of the commitment to cover the retirement contingency, this is a system established under a defined<br>contribution plan, for which the annual contributions to be made are fixed in advance.<br>By virtue of this commitment, the CEO is entitled to receive a retirement benefit when he/she reaches the legally<br>established retirement age. This benefit will be the result of the sum of the contributions made by CaixaBank and<br>their corresponding returns up to that date, provided that he/she is not terminated for just cause, and without<br>prejudice to the applicable treatment of discretionary pension benefits in accordance with the remuneration<br>regulations applicable to credit institutions.<br>With the termination of the CEO's contract, the contributions would be consolidated (except in the event of<br>termination for just cause attributable to the CEO) but in no case is there any provision for the possibility of receiving<br>an early retirement benefit, since its accrual and payment would occur only on the occasion and at the time of<br>retirement (or the occurrence of the other contingencies covered) and not on the occasion of the termination of the<br>contract.<br>The nature of these savings systems is not to indemnify or compensate for the loss of rights to the assumption of non<br>competition obligations, as they are configured as a savings system that is endowed over time with periodic<br>contributions and which form part of the fixed components of the usual remuneration package of the Executive<br>Directors; unlike indemnities or compensations for not competing, it grows over time and is not set in absolute terms.<br>Therefore, the institution would only be in breach of recommendation 64 if the mere consolidation of savings scheme<br>entitlements, without actual accrual or payment at the time of termination, were to be included in the concept of<br>termination payments or termination of contract payments as defined therein. |

### **E. Risk Control and Management Systems**

CAA - Consolidated Annual Accounts of the Group for 2023

*CAA - Consolidated Annual Accounts of the Group for 2023 CMR - Consolidated Management Report of the Group for 2023*

{402}------------------------------------------------

Auditor's report Information regarding the Internal Control System over Financial Reporting (ICSFR) 2023 financial year

{403}------------------------------------------------

Image /page/403/Picture/0 description: The image shows the logo for PwC, a multinational professional services network. The logo consists of the lowercase letters "pwc" in a bold, sans-serif font, positioned to the left of a graphic element. The graphic element is composed of several overlapping squares and rectangles in shades of orange and yellow, creating an abstract, modern design. A small, dark red rectangle is positioned just below the graphic element and above the "pwc" text.

*This version of our report is a free translation of the original, which was prepared in Spanish. All possible care has been taken to ensure that the translation is an accurate representation of the original. However, in all matters of interpretation of information, views or opinions, the original language version of our report takes precedence over this translation.*

# Auditor's report on "Information regarding the Internal Control System over Financial Reporting (ICSFR)"

To the administrators of CaixaBank, S.A.:

In accordance with the request of the Board of Directors of CaixaBank, S.A. ("the Company") and our engagement letter dated 15 December 2023, we have applied certain procedures in respect of the attached "Information regarding the ICSFR", included in section F of the Annual Corporate Governance Report of CaixaBank, S.A. for the 2023 financial year, which includes a summary of the Company's internal control procedures relating to its annual financial information.

The administrators are responsible for adopting the necessary measures to reasonably ensure the implementation, maintenance and supervision of an appropriate internal control system, and for developing improvements to that system and preparing and establishing the content of the accompanying Information regarding the ICSFR.

In this regard, it should be borne in mind that, regardless of the quality of the design and operating efficiency of the internal control system used by the Company in relation to its annual financial information, only a reasonable, but not absolute, degree of assurance may be obtained in relation to the objectives it seeks to achieve, due to the limitations inherent in any internal control system.

In the course of our audit work on the consolidated annual accounts and in accordance with Spanish Auditing Standards, the sole purpose of our evaluation of the Company's internal control system is to enable us to establish the scope, nature and timing of our audit procedures in respect of the Company's annual accounts. Accordingly, our internal control evaluation, performed for the purposes of our audit, is not sufficient in scope to enable us to issue a specific opinion on the effectiveness of such internal control over the regulated annual financial information.

For the purposes of the present report, we have exclusively applied the specific procedures described below, as indicated in the "*Guidelines concerning the auditor's Report on the Information regarding the Internal Control System over Financial Reporting for listed entities*" published by the National Securities Market Commission (hereinafter NSMC) on its web site, which sets out the work to be performed, the scope of such work and the content of this report. In view of the fact that, in any event, the scope of the work resulting from these procedures is reduced and substantially less than the scope of an audit or review of the internal control system, we do not express an opinion on the effectiveness thereof, its design or operational efficiency, in relation to the Company's annual financial information for the 2023 financial year described in the accompanying Information regarding the ICSFR. Had we applied additional procedures to those determined by the aforementioned Guidelines, or had we performed an audit or review of the internal control system in relation to the regulated annual financial information, other matters could have come to light in respect of which you would have been informed.

*PricewaterhouseCoopers Auditores, S.L., Pº de la Alameda, 35 Bis, 46023 Valencia, España Tel.: +34 963 036 900 / +34 902 021 111, Fax: +34 963 036 901*, www.pwc.es 1

{404}------------------------------------------------

Image /page/404/Picture/1 description: The image shows the logo for PwC, a multinational professional services network. The logo consists of the lowercase letters "pwc" in a bold, sans-serif font, positioned to the left of a graphic element. The graphic element is composed of a series of overlapping squares and rectangles in shades of orange and red, creating a layered, abstract design.

In addition, provided that this special work neither constitutes an account audit it is not even submitted to the governing regulations of audit, we do not express an opinion of audit in the terms foreseen in the mentioned regulation.

The Procedures applied were as follows:

- 1) Reading and understanding the information prepared by the Company in relation to the ICSFR as disclosed in the Directors' Report – and the evaluation of whether such information includes all the information required as per the minimum content set out in Section F regarding the description of the ICSFR, in the model of the Annual Corporate Governance Report, as established in Circular 5/2013 of the NSMC, dated June 12, 2013, and subsequent amendments, the most recent being Circular 3/2021, of September 28, of the NSMC (from now on the Circulars of NSMC).
- 2) Making enquiries of personnel in charge of preparing the information mentioned in point 1 above in order to: (i) obtain an understanding of the preparation process; (ii) obtain information that enables us to assess whether the terminology used is in line with the framework of reference; (iii) obtain information as to whether the control procedures described have been implemented and are functioning in the Company.
- 3) Review of supporting documentation explaining the information described in point 1 above and which mainly comprises the information made directly available to the persons responsible for preparing the information on the ICSFR. Such documentation includes reports prepared by the internal audit function, senior management and other internal and external specialists in support of the functions of the Audit and Control Committee.
- 4) Comparison of the information described in point 1 above with our knowledge of the Company´s ICSFR, obtained by means of the application of the procedures performed within the framework of the audit engagement on the consolidated annual accounts.
- 5) Reading the minutes of meetings of the Board of Directors, Audit and Control Committee and other committees of the Company, for the purposes of evaluating the consistency between the matters dealt with therein in relation to the ICSFR and the information described in point 1 above.
- 6) Obtaining a representation letter concerning the work performed, duly signed by the persons responsible for the preparation and drafting of the information mentioned in point 1 above.

As a result of the procedures applied in relation to the Information regarding the ICSFR, no inconsistencies or incidents have been identified which could affect such information.

This report has been prepared exclusively within the framework of the requirements of article 540 of the revised Spanish Companies Act and by the Circulars of de NSMC, for the purposes of describing the ICSFR in Annual Corporate Governance Reports.

PricewaterhouseCoopers Auditores, S.L.

**PRICEWATERHOUSECOOPERS AUDITORES, S.L.**

Original in Spanish signed by Raúl Ara Navarro

February 16, 2024

| EXECUTIVE DIRECTORS                           |                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

| Name or corporate<br>name of the director     | Position held in the<br>company<br>of society: | Profile                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| MR JOSÉ IGNACIO<br>GOIRIGOLZARRI<br>TELLAECHE | Executive Chairman                             | José Ignacio Goirigolzarri, was born in Bilbao in 1954. He has been the<br>Executive Chairman of CaixaBank since 2021. Holds a degree in<br>Economics and Business Studies from the Commercial University of<br>Deusto. Finances and Strategic Planning from the University of Leeds<br>(UK). Lecturer at the Commercial University of Deusto, in the Strategic<br>Planning Area (1977-1979). He joined Banco de Bilbao and in 1994 became<br>a member of BBV's Management Committee, responsible for<br>Commercial Banking in Spain and Latin American operations. In 2001 he<br>was appointed CEO of the BBVA Group, a position he held until October<br>2009. In May 2012, he was elected Chairman of Bankia and its parent<br>company, BFA, serving as such until March 2021, when Bankia merged<br>with CaixaBank. He is currently appointed Executive Chairman of<br>CaixaBank. He has been Director and Deputy Chairman of Telefónica and<br>Repsol, as well as Chairman of the Spain-USA Foundation, Director of<br>BBVA Bancomer in Mexico and Director of Citic Bank in China. He is<br>currently Chairman of CaixaBank, Deputy Chairman of CECA, Chairman<br>of FEDEA, Deputy Chairman of COTEC, Deputy Chairman of Fundación<br>FAD, Chairman of Deusto Business School, Chairman of CaixaBank<br>Dualiza and Chairman of Fundación Garum. |
| MR GONZALO<br>GORTAZAR<br>ROTAECHE            | CEO                                            | Born in Madrid in 1965, he has been the CEO of CaixaBank since June<br>2014. Gonzalo Gortazar holds a degree in Law and Business from<br>Universidad Pontificia de Comillas (ICADE) and an MBA with distinction<br>from the INSEAD Business School. He is also currently Chairman of<br>CaixaBank Payments & Consumer and Director of Banco BPI. He was the<br>Chief Financial Officer of CaixaBank until his appointment as CEO in June<br>2014. He was formerly the CEO of Criteria CaixaCorp from 2009 to June<br>2011. From 1993 to 2009 he worked at Morgan Stanley                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

| MR TOMÁS<br>MUNIESA<br>ARANTEGUI        | LA CAIXA BANKING<br>FOUNDATION                                                                           | Tomás Muniesa, born in Barcelona in 1952; he has been the Deputy<br>Chairman of CaixaBank since April 2018. He holds a degree in Business<br>Studies and a Master of Business Administration from the ESADE Business<br>School. He joined 'La Caixa' in 1976, and was appointed Deputy General<br>Manager in 1992. In 2011, he was appointed General Manager of Insurance<br>and Asset Management of CaixaBank, where he remained until November<br>2018. He was the Executive Deputy Chairman and CEO of VidaCaixa from<br>1997 to November 2018. He currently holds the positions of Deputy<br>Chairman of CaixaBank, VidaCaixa and SegurCaixa Adeslas. He is also a<br>member of the Trust of the ESADE Foundation and Director of Allianz<br>Portugal. Prior to this, he was Chairman of MEFF (Sociedad Rectora de<br>Productos Derivados), Deputy Chairman of BME (Bolsas y Mercados<br>Españoles), Second Deputy Chairman of UNESPA, Director and Chairman<br>of the Audit Commission of the Insurance Compensation Consortium,<br>Board Member of Vithas Sanidad SL and Substitute Board Member of<br>Grupo Financiero Inbursain Mexico.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| MS MARÍA TERESA<br>SANTERO<br>QUINTILLÁ | FROB Y BFA<br>TENEDORA DE<br>ACCIONES, S.A.U.                                                            | Teresa Santero was born in Camporrells (Huesca) in 1959. She has been a<br>member of the CaixaBank Board of Directors since 2021. She holds a<br>degree in Business Administration from the University of Zaragoza and a<br>PhD in Economics from the University of Illinois Chicago (USA). She has<br>been a lecturer at the UIE Business School in Madrid since 2012. Previously,<br>she held management positions in the Central Administration (General<br>Secretary for Industry in the Ministry of Industry, Trade and Tourism from                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

| MR JOSÉ SERNA<br>MASIÁ                  | LA CAIXA BANKING<br>FOUNDATION                                                                           | José Serna Masiá (Albacete, 1942) has been a member of CaixaBank's<br>Board of Directors since July 2016. He graduated in Law at the<br>Complutense University of Madrid in 1964, and began his career in legal<br>counselling with Butano, S.A. (1969/70). In 1971, he became a State Attorney,<br>providing services at the State Attorney's Office for Salamanca and at the<br>Ministries for Education and Science and Finance. He then joined the<br>Adversary Proceedings Department of the State at the Audiencia<br>Territorial de Madrid (now the Tribunal Superior de Justicia - High Court of<br>Justice), before taking leave of absence in 1983. From 1983 to 1987 he was<br>legal counsel to the Madrid Stock Exchange. In 1987, he became a<br>stockbroker at Barcelona Stock Exchange and was appointed secretary of<br>its Governing Body. He took part in the stock market reform of 1988 as<br>Chairman of the company that developed the new Barcelona Stock<br>Exchange and also as a member of the Advisory Committee to the<br>recently created Comisión Nacional del Mercado de Valores, the Spanish<br>securities market regulator. In 1989, he was elected Chairman of the<br>Barcelona Stock Exchange, a role that he held for two consecutive terms<br>until 1993. From 1991 to 1992, he was Chairman of the Spanish Sociedad de<br>Bolsas (Stock Exchange Company), which groups the four Spanish stock<br>exchanges together, and Deputy Chairman of the Spanish Financial<br>Futures Market, in Barcelona. He was also Deputy Chairman of Fundación<br>Barcelona Centro Financiero and of Sociedad de Valores y Bolsa |

He was also<br>a director of the companies ENDESA Diversificación and ENDESA Europa.<br>He worked as a notary in Barcelona from 2002 through to 2013.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

| Name or corporate<br>name of Director      | Profile | MR EDUARDO<br>JAVIER SANCHIZ<br>IRAZU      | Eduardo Javier Sanchiz Irazu, born in Vitoria in 1956, and has been a member of the Board of<br>Directors of CaixaBank since September 2017 and the Lead Director since 2023. He holds a degree in<br>economics from the University of Deusto, San Sebastián campus, and a Master's Degree in Business<br>Administration from the Instituto Empresa in Madrid. He was CEO of Almirall from July 2011 until 30<br>September 2017. During this period, the company underwent a significant strategic transformation<br>with the aim of becoming a global leader in skin treatment. Previously, after jointing Almirall in May<br>2004, he was executive director of Corporate Development and Finance and Chief Financial Officer.<br>In both positions, Eduardo led the company's international expansion through a number of alliances<br>with other companies, and through licensing of external products, in addition to five acquisitions of<br>companies and product portfolios. He also coordinated the IPO process in 2007. He was a member of<br>the Almirall Board of Directors from January 2005 and member of the Dermatology Committee from<br>its creation in 2015. Prior to joining Almirall, he worked for 22 years (17 outside Spain) at Eli Lilly & Co,<br>an American pharmaceutical company, in finance, marketing, sales and general management<br>positions. He was able to live in six different countries and some of his significant positions include<br>General Manager in Belgium, General Manager in Mexico and, in his last position in the company,<br>Executive Officer for the business area that encompasses countries in the centre, north, east and<br>south of Europe. He was a member of the American Chamber of Commerce in Mexico and of the<br>Association of Pharmaceutical Industries in a number of countries in Europe and Latin America. He is<br>a member of the Board of Directors of the French pharmaceutical company Pierre Fabre, S.A. and a<br>member of its Strategy Committee and its Audit Committee. He is also a member of the Board of<br>Directors of the venture capital company Sabadell Asabys Health Innovation Investments 2B S.C.R.,<br>S.A.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
